title: Semana Santa 2026: CJNG Shows Tactical Restraint in Tourist Zones; Security Forces Hold Coastal Corridors
detail: Day 9 of Operativo Semana Santa Segura 2026 (March 27–April 12). Federal and state security forces maintain elevated posture across CJNG-controlled coastal tourist destinations including Puerto Vallarta, Manzanillo, and Mazatlán. CJNG has demonstrated its historically consistent pattern of tactical restraint during major tourist holidays in zones where the cartel has financial stakes in the hospitality and timeshare sectors — consistent with InSight Crime's documented 'business protection' behavior in tourist plazas. No cartel-attributed violence reported at major CJNG-controlled Pacific Coast destinations during Semana Santa weekend. Quintana Roo, Jalisco, Colima, and Nayarit state governments reported record domestic tourism figures at coastal destinations. Security analysts note the calm reflects CJNG's calculated self-interest rather than any meaningful degradation of the cartel's operational presence or capability in these zones.

title: Operativo Enjambre Sustained Pressure: 64+ Officials Detained, CJNG Municipal Networks Under Stress
detail: Operativo Enjambre — the Mexican government's sustained anti-corruption campaign targeting officials with alleged CJNG ties — continues at high operational tempo into April 2026. Cumulative arrest total holds at 64+ officials detained across Jalisco, Michoacán, Guerrero, Morelos, and additional states since the February 22 El Mencho operation. The campaign has systematically targeted CJNG's municipal-level protection architecture: mayors, police chiefs, customs officials, and municipal employees whose corruption enabled the cartel's free movement across key corridors. Security Secretary García Harfuch has described Operativo Enjambre as a 'second phase' of the Tapalpa operation — targeting not just CJNG gunmen but the institutional enablers that allowed El Mencho to operate for decades. The Tequila, Jalisco mayor arrested March 29 represents the highest-profile municipal target to date. No new Enjambre arrests publicly confirmed for April 4.
